Now the core analysis: compute elapsed business hours (9–17 UTC, weekdays) per assignment, normalize by role and task complexity, and rank employees."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1614,"cache_read_input_tokens":26369,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1614,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":48,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"f7306a62-f352-4988-9f9b-09816b5e2e08","uuid":"e86d2490-14c3-45f1-b274-dd0c2a152ce8","request_id":"req_011CbwRFwWKJFXTAHeS9L1Ai"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-fable-5","id":"msg_01QtemVRGypbLdV4CXAcuaWk","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01HkPEtzoDihVR33zxW3Nx7f","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, glob, numpy as np\n\ndef business_hours(start, end):\n    \"\"\"Vectorized business hours (9-17 UTC, Mon-Fri) between two datetime series.\"\"\"\n    s = start.copy(); e = end.copy()\n    out = np.zeros(len(s))\n    # iterate days is slow; use formula: count full business days between, plus partials\n    # simpler: per-row loop with numpy on dates (15k rows fine)\n    res = []\n    for a, b in zip(s, e):\n        if b <= a: res.append(0.0); continue\n        total = 0.0\n        days = pd.date_range(a.normalize(), b.normalize(), freq='D')\n        for d in days:\n            if d.dayofweek >= 5: continue\n            ws = d + pd.Timedelta(hours=9); we = d + pd.Timedelta(hours=17)\n            lo = max(a, ws); hi = min(b, we)\n            if hi > lo: total += (hi-lo).total_seconds()/3600\n        res.append(total)\n    return np.array(res)\n\nta = pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f, parse_dates=['assigned_at','unassigned_at']) for f in sorted(glob.glob('task_assignment_*.csv'))], ignore_index=True)\ntasks = pd.read_csv('task_001.csv', parse_dates=['created_at','completed_at'])\nta = ta.merge(tasks[['task_id','complexity_score','task_type','priority']], on='task_id', how='left')\nta['bh'] = business_hours(ta['assigned_at'], ta['unassigned_at'])\nprint(ta.groupby('assignment_role')['bh'].describe()[['count','mean','50%','max']])\nta.to_pickle('/tmp/ta.pkl')\n\n# does duration scale with complexity?\nprint(ta.groupby(['assignment_role','complexity_score'])['bh'].median().unstack().round(1))\nEOF","description":"Compute business-hours durations per assignment"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1614,"cache_read_input_tokens":26369,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1614,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":48,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"f7306a62-f352-4988-9f9b-09816b5e2e08","uuid":"1092c74d-2225-4e43-b58b-20662223a742","request_id":"req_011CbwRFwWKJFXTAHeS9L1Ai"}

<< 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np\nta = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/ta.pkl').sort_values(['task_id','assigned_at'])\n\ndef bh_vec(start, end):\n    res = []\n    for a, b in zip(start, end):\n        if pd.isna(a) or pd.isna(b) or b <= a: res.append(0.0); continue\n        t = 0.0\n        for d in pd.date_range(a.normalize(), b.normalize(), freq='D'):\n            if d.dayofweek >= 5: continue\n            lo = max(a, d + pd.Timedelta(hours=9)); hi = min(b, d + pd.Timedelta(hours=17))\n            if hi > lo: t += (hi-lo).total_seconds()/3600\n        res.append(t)\n    return np.array(res)\n\n# pickup gap: time between previous stage end and this assignment start\nta['prev_end'] = ta.groupby('task_id')['unassigned_at'].shift(1)\nta['gap_bh'] = bh_vec(ta['prev_end'], ta['assigned_at'])\nhas_prev = ta['prev_end'].notna()\ngmed = ta[has_prev].groupby('assignment_role')['gap_bh'].transform('median')\nta.loc[has_prev,'gap_ratio'] = ta.loc[has_prev,'gap_bh'] / gmed.clip(lower=0.1)\n\ngg = ta[has_prev].groupby('employee_id').agg(n=('gap_bh','size'), med_gap=('gap_bh','median'), mean_gap=('gap_bh','mean'))\nprint(\"Top pickup-gap employees (median gap business-hrs):\")\nprint(gg[gg.n>=10].sort_values('med_gap', ascending=False).head(12).round(2).to_string())\n\n# cross-check with code_review and approval response times\ncr = pd.read_csv('code_review_001.csv', parse_dates=['requested_at','completed_at'])\ncr['bh'] = bh_vec(cr['requested_at'], cr['completed_at'])\ncrg = cr.groupby('reviewer_id').agg(n=('bh','size'), med=('bh','median'))\nprint(\"\\nSlowest code reviewers:\")\nprint(crg[crg.n>=5].sort_values('med', ascending=False).head(8).round(2).to_string())\n\nap = pd.read_csv('approval_001.csv', parse_dates=['requested_at','resolved_at'])\nap['bh'] = bh_vec(ap['requested_at'], ap['resolved_at'])\napg = ap.groupby('approver_id').agg(n=('bh','size'), med=('bh','median'))\nprint(\"\\nSlowest << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np\nta = pd.read_pickle('/tmp/ta2.pkl').sort_values(['task_id','assigned_at'])\nta['prev_emp'] = ta.groupby('task_id')['employee_id'].shift(1)\nsub = ta[ta['prev_end'].notna()].copy()\n\n# attribution test: does the gap follow the NEXT assignee or the PREVIOUS employee?\nnxt = sub.groupby('employee_id')['gap_bh'].median()\nprv = sub.groupby('prev_emp')['gap_bh'].median()\nprint('Variance of per-person median gap — next-assignee attribution:', round(nxt.var(),2),\n      '| prev-employee attribution:', round(prv.var(),2))\nprint('Top by prev-employee:', prv.sort_values(ascending=False).head(8).round(1).to_dict())\n\n# for the 8 gap suspects: is the gap high regardless of who the previous employee was?\nfor e in [228,325,26,45,74,302,204,81]:\n    s = sub[sub.employee_id==e]\n    print(f'emp {e}: n={len(s)}, med_gap={s.gap_bh.median():.1f}, distinct prev emps={s.prev_emp.nunique()}, roles={s.assignment_role.value_counts().to_dict()}')\n\n# seniority-normalized gap z-scores\nemp = pd.read_csv('employee_001.csv')\ngg = sub.groupby('employee_id').agg(n=('gap_bh','size'), med_gap=('gap_bh','median')).query('n>=8')\ngg = gg.join(emp.set_index('employee_id')['seniority_level'])\ngg['z'] = gg.groupby('seniority_level')['med_gap'].transform(lambda x:(x-x.median())/(x.quantile(.75)-x.quantile(.25)).clip(0.1))\nprint('\\nTop seniority-normalized gap outliers:')\nprint(gg.sort_values('z', ascending=False).head(14).round(2).to_string())\nEOF","description":"Test gap attribution and seniority-normalized quick per-employee check\nbase = span[~span.has_suspect]['cal_days'].median()\nfor e in sorted(SUSPECTS):\n    m = span[span['emps'].apply(lambda s: e in s)]['cal_days'].median()\n    n = span['emps'].apply(lambda s: e in s).sum()\n    print(f'emp {e}: tasks={n}, median task span={m:.1f}d (no-suspect baseline {base:.1f}d)')\n\n# check borderline non-suspects for contrast\nfor e in [259,251,120,51,5,232]:\n    m = span[span['emps'].apply(lambda s: e in s)]['cal_days'].median()\n    n = span['emps'].apply(lambda s: e in s).sum()\n    print(f'ctrl {e}: tasks={n}, median task span={m:.1f}d')\nEOF","description":"Validate suspects via end-to-end task durations"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3480,"cache_read_input_tokens":37704,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":3480,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":33,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"f7306a62-f352-4988-9f9b-09816b5e2e08","uuid":"5f04b790-c5c1-4ce8-81de-aeb1a8aff624","request_id":"req_011CbwRRoKDsCnnmK7uAjaNg"}

The answer is in `/app/answer.json`: **employees 26, 45, 74, 81, 123, 204, 222, 228, 281, 282, 302, 325**.\n\n## How I identified them\n\nI computed every assignment's duration in business hours (9:00–17:00 UTC, weekdays) from the task assignment tables, then measured two distinct failure modes:\n\n**Slow execution (poor performance)** — actual stage duration vs. the peer median for the same role and task complexity. Four employees take **4.5–5.4× the peer median** on their own stages, while the next-highest employee is at 1.45×, a huge break:\n- **123** (mid software engineer) and **222** (mid platform engineer) — slow on implementation/ownership work\n- **281** (security manager) and **282** (platform manager) — slowest code reviewers (~10 business hrs vs ~1.4 typical) and slowest approvers in the company\n\n**Slow pickup (overload)** — business hours work sat in queue between the previous stage finishing and the employee starting. The population median is ~3 business hours; a separate cluster of eight employees sits at **11–23 hours** (next-highest non-suspect: 6.5): **228, 325, 26, 45, 74, 302, 204, 81**. Their overload is visible in the data — 228 logged 170 meeting-hours vs. a 38-hour peer average, 302 logged 106, and 26, 45, 74, 81, 204, 325 carry message volumes 2–3× their seniority peers.\n\n**Validation.** Three checks confirm the set: (1) the queue delay follows the assignee, not their upstream collaborators — variance of per-person median gap is 4× higher under assignee attribution, and each suspect's delay persists across 29–66 distinct previous coworkers; (2) the same names top the independent code-review and approval response-time tables; (3) end-to-end, tasks touching any suspect take a median of 7–19 calendar days vs. a 5.2-day baseline for suspect-free tasks — and this holds even on tasks where a suspect is the only flagged participant, while borderline non-suspects fall back to baseline once shared tasks with suspects are excluded."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":502,"cache_read_input_tokens":44571,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":502,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":2,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"f7306a62-f352-4988-9f9b-09816b5e2e08","uuid":"ccafb005-d43d-44a8-aab6-7dfcc016ea85","request_id":"req_011CbwRVZqDhLdywK4GjCP1e"}
